title: Backtracking, Constraint Propagation, and Branch-and-Bound · Cost-Model Clinic: Pruning Soundness
summary: Search schedules and assignments while proving why pruned branches cannot help. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 pruning soundness increment in a bounded combinatorial solver with pruning audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.
Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
